nodes.name: When Executed by WF-001
nodes.notes: Input data mode = 'Accept all data'. In the exported JSON this is inputSource: 'passthrough' (LOWERCASE t — verified against a real n8n export; the camelCase 'passThrough' I previously authored is not the value n8n emits). Receives the full payload from WF-001 including binary.

nodes.name: Load OCR Provider Settings

parameters.query: SELECT settings FROM configuration.providers WHERE kind='ocr';

nodes.notes: Runtime endpoint/model/timeout (R-08). Bind CRIE Postgres credential.

nodes.name: Re-attach Binary (pattern)
nodes.notes: PATTERN — restore binary after a Postgres node that precedes a binary consumer. Any future SW workflow with Postgres-before-binary-consumer must apply this same re-attach-from-trigger step. See CRIE Workflow Engineering Guidelines.

nodes.name: submit() — POST Analyze

parameters.url: ={{ $('Load OCR Provider Settings').first().json.settings.endpoint }}/documentintelligence/documentModels/{{ $('Load OCR Provider Settings').first().json.settings.model }}:analyze?api-version=2024-11-30

nodes.notes: SUBMIT adapter (Azure). POST :analyze with the binary. fullResponse=true so the Operation-Location header is available. neverError=true so 202 is not treated as an error. Node-level retry handles transient 429/503 only — NOT operation progress. Auth: Generic Credential Type -> Header Auth (httpHeaderAuth). Credential must be re-bound in the n8n UI after import (n8n does not preserve credential IDs across imports).

nodes.name: submit() — capture pollUrl
nodes.notes: Captures the poll URL (Azure: Operation-Location) using provider config (settings.poll.pollUrlHeader), honors Retry-After for the first wait, and seeds the poll budget (maxAttempts / maxElapsedMs). A non-202 or missing header is a terminal submit failure.

nodes.name: poll() — wait
nodes.notes: Waits the interval computed by the poll adapter (honors Retry-After; default ~2s per Azure guidance).

nodes.name: poll() — GET operation
nodes.notes: GET the operation URL. fullResponse for Retry-After header. Node retry = transient faults only. Auth: Generic Credential Type -> Header Auth (httpHeaderAuth). Credential must be re-bound in the n8n UI after import (n8n does not preserve credential IDs across imports).

nodes.name: poll() — evaluate
nodes.notes: Normalises the provider poll response to {status,result} via config (statusPath, terminalSuccess/Failure, resultPath). Honors Retry-After, backs off on 429, increments the attempt counter, and enforces maxAttempts/maxElapsedMs so the loop can never run forever.

nodes.name: terminal?
nodes.notes: true -> terminal (succeeded/failed/timeout). false -> loop back to Wait and poll again. Schema is a verbatim copy of a real n8n IF v2 export (nested parameters.conditions object); only the expression/value were substituted.

nodes.name: succeeded?
nodes.notes: succeeded -> normalize(). failed/timeout -> ocr=null so WF-001's §154 OCR-fail path fires. Schema is a verbatim copy of a real n8n IF v2 export.

nodes.name: normalize() — Normalized OCR Model
nodes.notes: TEMPORARY verification log at line 1 (remove once confirmed). Reads $json as the canonical input: the IF gates (terminal? + succeeded?) already proved this item is the terminal successful poll. NO $('poll() — evaluate') history lookups remain.

nodes.name: shapeResult() — OCR failed
nodes.notes: Emits ocr=null + ocrError so WF-001's node-if-ocr routes to the §154 HUMAN_REVIEW path. Reads the item directly; no run-history lookup.

meta.note: Provider call via HTTP Request node (portable). Credentials bound at import: azureApi + CRIE Postgres.
meta.enrichment: output = {...incomingItem, newResult}; never discards accumulated state
meta.jsonbFix: settings is jsonb (object); removed JSON.parse on settings; LLM-content parse retained
meta.triggerFix: executeWorkflowTrigger upgraded to 1.1 inputSource=passThrough for binary passthrough
meta.binaryReattach: Pattern node added: Postgres-before-binary-consumer -> restore binary from trigger by node reference
meta.lro: Azure LRO in-workflow poll loop: submit() -> poll() (Wait+GET+evaluate, Retry-After honored, 429 backoff, attempt/elapsed budget) -> shapeResult(). Output contract unchanged.
changelog: v2.1.0: shapeResult now carries Azure's real paragraph structure into ocr.pages[].paragraphs (with ocr.paragraphSource flag). Additive — existing contract fields unchanged.

v2.2.0: FIX — shapeResult/fail-out read the TERMINAL poll iteration via $('poll() — evaluate').all() instead of $json. In the Wait-node loop, $json could resolve to a stale iteration whose _result was null, producing ocr.pages=[] and ocr.raw={} downstream. Also throws if analyzeResult has no pages, instead of silently emitting an empty OCR object.

v2.3.0: ROOT-CAUSE FIX. $('poll() — evaluate').all() returns items of ONE run only (runIndex defaults to 0), so the previous terminalPoll() saw a single stale 'running' iteration with _result=null and fell back to it -> 'analyzeResult carried no pages (status=running)'. Now walks EVERY run index (0..runIndex) per the n8n canonical loop pattern, selects the terminal run, and throws rather than falling back to stale data. poll-eval logs each iteration.

v3.0.0: OCR NORMALIZER (Option 1, paragraph-first). Root cause of the 1-page result: DOCX has no native pagination, so Azure returns pages.length=1 with all 10,729 paragraphs in paragraphs[]; the old page-centric shaper read pages[0].lines and discarded the document. The normalizer now emits a format-aware model: paragraphs[] primary (page:null for docx, real page for pdf/image), pages[] empty for docx (never synthesized), plus content/stats/raw. Format detected from mimeType/filename.

v3.1.0: normalized contract gains tables[] and figures[]. Tables were previously COUNTED and their structure discarded (143 present in the CUBES SRS). Figures preserve id/page/caption/element-refs/geometry with imageAvailable=false; figure image bytes need an explicit output=figures request that is not currently sent. Both arrays are [] when absent. Stats gain figures/tableCells.

v3.2.0: (a) ROOT CAUSE FIX — 'terminal?' and 'succeeded?' rebuilt from a REAL n8n IF v2 export (parameters.conditions is a nested OBJECT {options, conditions[], combinator}, plus a separate top-level options:{}). The previously hand-authored FLAT shape imported with ZERO conditions, so both IF nodes evaluated vacuously TRUE and passed every non-terminal poll straight to normalize() — the loop never looped. Operators are plain 'equals' (no unary true/singleValue). typeVersion 2.2. (b) Trigger inputSource corrected to lowercase 'passthrough' (Accept all data) per real export. (c) Azure HTTP nodes switched to Generic Credential Type -> Header Auth (httpHeaderAuth); credential is re-bound manually after import.

v3.3.0: normalize() and the OCR-fail node now read the item on the wire ($json) instead of reconstructing loop history via $('poll() — evaluate').all(0,r). MEASURED on n8n 2.29.9: that reference returns only the FIRST run of a Wait-loop to a downstream Code node — execution history showed run1=running/run2=succeeded, the IF gates correctly routed run 2, yet the reference still yielded ['running'] across 1 run. The IF gates (terminal? + succeeded?) already prove the incoming item is the terminal successful poll, so no history lookup is needed. Throws if a non-terminal item reaches normalize().

v3.3.1: temporary first-line verification log in normalize() reporting the incoming item's _poll.status / _poll.terminal / _result presence / pages / paragraphs. Confirms $json is the terminal successful poll before permanently relying on it. History lookups were already removed in v3.3.0; the only remaining mention is an explanatory comment.
